macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Work for workplace automation
items.summary: OpenAI unveiled ChatGPT Work, an agent designed to execute tasks across different applications and files, intensifying competition in professional AI tools. The launch underscores a broader industry push toward AI agents that can handle end-to-end office workflows.

items.title: OpenAI launches GPT-Live, new real-time voice models
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Live, a new family of voice models that can listen and speak simultaneously in real time. The models are designed to improve natural conversation and live voice interactions in ChatGPT.

items.title: Meta opens developer access to Muse Spark 1.1
items.summary: Meta released public developer access to its Muse Spark AI model and an upgraded 1.1 version. The move positions Meta more directly against Anthropic and OpenAI in monetizing AI model access.

items.title: xAI launches Grok 4.5 for coding and agentic tasks
items.summary: xAI released Grok 4.5, describing it as its most intelligent model yet and aimed at coding and agent-style tasks. The launch adds to the rapid cycle of frontier-model releases among leading AI labs.

items.title: Meta plans first AI chip manufacturing and major compute expansion
items.summary: Reuters reported that Meta plans to begin manufacturing an AI chip from September as part of a broader effort to raise computing power to 14 gigawatts next year. The company is also building a large data center in Alberta, its first in Canada, to support AI infrastructure growth.

items.title: Anthropic adds Ben Bernanke to Long-Term Benefit Trust
items.summary: Anthropic appointed former U.S. Federal Reserve chair Ben Bernanke to its Long-Term Benefit Trust, the oversight body tasked with keeping the company aligned with its public mission. The appointment highlights continued emphasis on governance and safety oversight in advanced AI labs.

items.title: Cognition releases SWE-1.7, claiming strong coding performance at lower cost
items.summary: Cognition launched SWE-1.7, saying it is its most capable model yet and can run at 1,000 tokens per second. The company says it approaches top-tier benchmark performance while reducing cost, reflecting the growing race on efficiency as well as capability.

items.title: AI adoption shifts toward internal champions as enterprises scale usage
items.summary: Industry coverage highlighted companies increasingly using internal AI champions to drive adoption, with BCG reporting that 74% of front-line employees now use AI regularly, up from 51% in 2025. The trend suggests AI deployment is moving from pilot projects into broader workplace integration.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 1 and 2 arrive on PS4 and PS5
items.summary: Activision has released Call of Duty: Black Ops and Call of Duty: Black Ops 2 on PlayStation 4 and PlayStation 5, though IGN notes the ports are priced at a premium. The launch is one of the biggest new game-release stories in the current news cycle.

items.title: Pokémon Go marks its 10th anniversary with a livestream
items.summary: IGN reports that Pokémon Go is celebrating its 10th anniversary with a special livestream covering announcements and surprises. The event makes it one of the notable mobile and live-service game updates in the last day.

items.title: GTA Online’s next update is set for next week
items.summary: IGN says GTA Online’s next major update, The Kortz Center Heist, is scheduled to release next week. The report also notes that fans have already spotted Rockstar-related Easter eggs tied to the content.

items.title: Nintendo to shut down Mario Kart Tour this September
items.summary: IGN reports that Nintendo will end service for Mario Kart Tour in September after seven years. This is a significant mobile-gaming shutdown and one of the clearest industry-service developments in the past 24 hours.

items.title: DC K.O. announced as a new DC mobile fighting game
items.summary: Gaming news coverage on YouTube says Warner Bros. Interactive Entertainment and Drop Fake have announced DC K.O., a new DC fighting game for iOS and Android. The game is scheduled for 2027, with pre-registration already open.

items.title: Dinoblade launches on Steam
items.summary: The same gaming news roundup notes that Dinoblade launched on Steam on July 24. It is one of the more concrete new game-release items highlighted in the last day.

items.title: Epic Games Store Free Game: Foure Tails
items.summary: The gaming news roundup says this week’s Epic Games Store free title is Foure Tails, a story-driven card game. The promotion is reported to run through July 30.

items.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced posts strong Steam launch
items.summary: IGN reports that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is off to a strong start on Steam, with around 100,000 concurrent players. The report says that is more than any other title in the franchise to date.

items.title: Battlefield 6 Season 4 dated for July 21
items.summary: VGTimes reports that EA and Battlefield Studios announced Season 4 for Battlefield 6, with the update arriving across PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X/S. The report also says a Top Gun crossover is planned for the season’s second phase on August 18.

items.title: Zenless Zone Zero Version 3.1 announced for July 29
items.summary: VGTimes reports that HoYoverse has announced Zenless Zone Zero Version 3.1, timed to the game’s second anniversary. The update is said to add new agents, a permanent mode, events, and rewards across supported platforms.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
